name: SponsorBlock
description: Skip sponsored segments in YouTube videos using SponsorBlock data.

versions.changelog: Fix force scan filter excluding Episode and Movie item kinds. Broaden IncludeItemTypes to include Episode and Movie alongside Video so library backfills cover all relevant media.

Add a one-shot Force Scan button for existing archives. Admins can backfill every video in the selected libraries once, while the normal daily refresh stays limited to tracked Pending and HasData items. Includes duplicate-scan protection and status endpoints.

Repair pruned SponsorBlock segments on playback. When Jellyfin refreshes a changed media file it can delete generated MediaSegment rows while the plugin state still says HasData; playback start now verifies that Jellyfin still has segment rows and re-fetches/recreates them if they were pruned.

Fix delayed SponsorBlock loading. Register the daily refresh scheduled task so Pending items are retried automatically, correct the 24-hour playback retry condition, and wait for playback-start processing so freshly fetched segments can reach the first playback session.

Actually fix segments not reaching clients. Jellyfin's IMediaSegmentManager.CreateSegmentAsync expects the second arg to be the MD5-derived provider id (new Guid(MD5(name.ToLowerInvariant() UTF-16 LE)).ToString("N")), not the literal provider name. We were passing "SponsorBlock" verbatim, so stored segments didn't match any registered provider and the public MediaSegments API filtered them out. Pre-compute the hash and pass that. After upgrading, hit Reset on the config page so old segments get re-fetched under the correct provider id.

Fix segments not reaching clients. v1.1.0.0 dropped the IMediaSegmentProvider registration to avoid the slow scan, but Jellyfin filters segments by registered provider, so stored segments were never served. Re-register a stub provider (Supports => false, GetMediaSegments => empty) so Jellyfin recognizes 'SponsorBlock' as a known provider while the orchestrator stays in charge of fetching.

Add admin Reset button on the plugin config page that wipes SponsorBlock state and owned segments for items in the configured library scope, so the orchestrator re-fetches on next playback. Useful for re-fetching items whose SponsorBlock data became available after the initial scan.

Replace media-segment scan with event-driven orchestrator. Add daily refresh task at configurable hour. Add per-library scoping (empty by default — admin must opt in). SQLite per-item state machine (Pending/HasData/NoData) with 48h sanity check.
Remove non-functional user segment actions table from config page.
